title: elaphoglossum wawrae
intro: Elaphoglossum wawrae, also known as the Wawra's elaphoglossum, is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Elaphoglossum wawrae include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and even exhibit potential antiviral properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an infusion or tincture for medicinal purposes, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential benefits and risks.
